rationale_for_baseline_tier: A sun-triggered, afebrile, nasolabial-sparing malar rash with no serositis/nephritis is a cutaneous lupus flare — low acuity, managed with cool compress, mild topical steroid and strict photoprotection. The only escalations are for facial cellulitis, an organ crisis, or ocular involvement.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Spreading warm swollen redness involving the nasolabial folds, pus, redness into the eyes with pain/vision loss, or fever
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads and becomes hot and swollen involving the creases beside your nose, spreads into your eyes with pain or vision changes, oozes pus, or you get a fever, go to the emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Sharp chest pain on breathing/breathlessness, or cola/red urine with ankle swelling
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get sharp chest pain on breathing or breathlessness, or your urine turns cola/red-coloured with swollen ankles, go to the emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Emphasise photoprotection and rheumatology linkage; know the organ-crisis red flags.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a sun-triggered lupus skin rash, not an organ attack — it skips the creases beside your nose and you have no fever, chest or kidney symptoms. Use a cool compress and mild steroid cream, avoid the sun tomorrow, and wear SPF 50+ daily. See your GP within a week. Go to ED for spreading hot swollen redness or pus, redness into your eyes, chest pain on breathing, or cola/red urine with swollen ankles.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within 7 days; ED for cellulitis/organ-crisis features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No after-hours care needed unless a red flag develops.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth impression of a cutaneous lupus (malar) flare; facial cellulitis and organ crisis (serositis/nephritis) excluded (nasolabial sparing, afebrile, no chest/renal features); cool compress/topical steroid/photoprotection advised; organ-crisis safety-net given; rheumatology review recommended.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without a cellulitis/organ-crisis screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A facial cellulitis (with orbital risk) or a lupus organ crisis is missed and progresses.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Antibiotics/ED for an afebrile nasolabial-sparing malar rash is over-triage.
